kept.text:
With $COIN earnings approaching, the options market is already pricing in expectations. 

Here’s the setup for your $CONY, the YieldMax® COIN Option Income Strategy ETF position: 

Breakdown: 

• The IV Spike: 30-day implied volatility sits at 75.41%, or 2.93% above its 180-day level. For comparison, it averaged 68.16% ahead of the last four earnings events. 
• The Expected Move: Current at-the-money straddle pricing suggests a move of approximately 11.60%. A straddle reflects the combined cost of a call and put at the same strike, commonly used to estimate expected volatility around earnings. 
• The Goal: The approach focuses on capturing these elevated premiums heading into earnings to help support income generation. 
• The Bar: Consensus expectations are $0.377 EPS (earnings per share, or profit per outstanding share) and $1.514B in revenue. 
• Positioning: In the event of a larger-than-expected move, call spreads are used to help maintain upside participation. 

With expectations already priced in, what’s your read on $COIN this time around?

profile_bio.description: I write about asymmetric investments that go unnoticed, with a deep focus on emerging markets.

quoted_tweet.text:
Portfolio has been in transition this year.

$HOOD $COIN were top 5 positions but are in the midst of being phased out.

In their place, $DLO $GLXY are my picks and are in the midst of becoming full positions. 

I’ve also started taking stakes in extremely durable compounders like $FICO $AMZN $TSM.

After back to back 100% years and a ~50% YTD return, I am now more concerned about reducing drawdowns, keeping gains. 

Of course, if there are asymmetric risk/reward opportunities in the market, I will take them.

kept.text:
$HOOD $COIN

I hate to say this, but I called exactly what is happening, and tried warning everyone.

When I made the post below, the majority of comments were negative, because we were in a bull market. I suspect many have the opposite view now.

“Be fearful when others are greedy, greedy when others are fearful” is easy to say but hard to execute.

profile_bio.description: I write about asymmetric investments that go unnoticed, with a deep focus on emerging markets.

quoted_tweet.text:
On Cyclicality & Holding Periods (with regards to $HOOD & $COIN):

My favourite holding period is forever. However, that simply isn’t possible with cyclical businesses.

The traditional cyclical sectors include oil&gas, homebuilders, automobiles, semiconductors etc…

However, in recent times, the most obviously cyclical industry has been cryptocurrencies.

Two of my key holdings happen to fall into that category: $HOOD $COIN

I believe both of these businesses are “generational” type businesses (yes I know that name gets thrown around way too easily, but i truly believe they are).

They have mission driven founders who have executed unlike any other in the space, incredible product market fit and strong tailwinds going for it.

However, there is no way to hide from the fact that Crypto operates in 4 year cycles, with a typical 80-90% drawdown to be expected in $BTC and even larger for other alts.

This will inevitably lead to a massive decrease in transaction-related revenues as trading demand falls off a cliff. 

$COIN of course is a pure crypto play, with ~80% of revenues today generated from transaction revenues, while $HOOD earns ~40% of its transaction-based revenues from Crypto. 

Both have also benefited heavily from net interest revenues due to rates staying higher for longer. 

$COIN earns ~20% of its revenue from interest earned through its cash/stablecoins/cryptocurrency holdings while roughly 1/3 of $HOOD revenues today are from net interest revenues.

It is inevitable that the 4 year crypto cycle, coupled with lower interest rates, will act as a major headwind against both businesses.

I guess, after this whole ramble, my point is that I believe both businesses will struggle in 2026, and possibly 2027 as it comps against 2025’s peak transaction and interest revenues.

Hence, I will likely sell off 80-100% of my stakes in both businesses, despite how much of a believer I am in them, with the hope of repurchasing them in 2026/2027 at trough multiples and when people start giving up on them (as I did in 2023, $COIN at $80 and $HOOD at $8)

As usual, NFA!
